Tests if an uninitialized variable is of type "undefined".

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of the `typeof` operator in JavaScript to check the type of an uninitialized variable. The variable `a` is declared but not assigned a value, making it `undefined`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the type of `a` is indeed `"undefined"`. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on a basic type-checking operation.
